source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
title: Ensure session Idle disconnect timeout is set to 10 minutes or less

input_row.source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
input_row.title: Ensure session Idle disconnect timeout is set to 10 minutes or less
input_row.description: Idle disconnect timeout in minutes is the amount of time that users can be inactive before they are disconnected from their streaming session and the Disconnect timeout in minutes time begins.
input_row.rationale: Users are considered idle when they stop providing keyboard or mouse input during their streaming session. File uploads and downloads, audio in, audio out, and pixels changing do not qualify as user activity. Once disconnected from their streaming session the Disconnect timeout begins.
input_row.audit: Perform the following steps to view the Fleet settings in AppStream From the Console 1. Log in to the AppStream 2.0 console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/appstream2 2. In the left pane click on Fleets. 3. Select the link for the fleet name you wish to view. 4. Scroll to the Fleet configuration section and confirm that Idle disconnect timeout is set to 10 minutes or less. If Idle disconnect timeout is set to anything greater than 10 minutes refer to the remediation below.
input_row.remediation: Perform the following steps to view the Fleet settings in AppStream From the Console 1. Log in to the AppStream 2.0 console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/appstream2 2. In the left pane click on Fleets. 3. Select the link for the fleet name you wish to view. 4. Scroll to the Fleet configuration section and click Edit 5. Change the Idle disconnect timeout to 5 minutes or less and click Save changes Internal Only - General
input_row.justification: While the first AI opinion suggests automation via boto3, the second opinion correctly points out that the session idle disconnect timeout for AWS WorkSpaces is not directly accessible through any current AWS API, CLI, or SDK method. This setting is managed via the AWS Console under WorkSpaces client properties and is not exposed programmatically for audit. Therefore, verification must be performed manually through the AWS Console.
input_row.manual_summary: 1. Log in to the AWS Management Console and navigate to the Amazon WorkSpaces service. 2. In the left navigation pane, select "Directories." 3. For each directory, click on the directory name to open its details. 4. Under the "Access Control Options" or "User Access" section, locate the "Client Properties" or "Session Settings." 5. Verify that the "Idle Disconnect Timeout" is set to 10 minutes (600 seconds) or less. 6. Repeat this process for all directories and WorkSpaces in your environment. 7. Document your findings, including screenshots or configuration details as evidence of compliance.
input_row.default_value: By default, AWS Appstream 2.0 idle disconnect time is15 minutes

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. Log in to the AWS Management Console and navigate to the Amazon AppStream 2.0 service.
2. In the left navigation pane, select "Fleets."
3. For each fleet, click on the fleet name to open its configuration details.
4. Scroll to the "Fleet configuration" section and locate the "Idle disconnect timeout" setting.
5. Verify that the "Idle disconnect timeout" is set to 10 minutes (600 seconds) or less.
6. Document your findings, including screenshots or configuration details as evidence of compliance.
